title: carmona retusa
intro: Carmona retusa, also known as the Camphor bush or Korean mint, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is native to Korea and parts of China, and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic qualities. The health benefits of Carmona retusa include relief from digestive issues, sore throats, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to reduce stress and anxiety. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the Carmona retusa plant are typically used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
